# docs/db/ENGINE_SCHEMA.md — the `engine` schema, WITNESSED PROD SNAPSHOT

**Snapshot taken:** 2026-07-15, founder-run in the Supabase SQL editor against `nvzkbagqxbysoeszxent` / `main` (PRODUCTION), role `postgres`. **25 tables, 242 columns.**
**Generated by:** `db/queries/engine_schema_dump.sql`. **Regenerate on demand by re-running that file.**
**NEVER HAND-EDIT.** A hand-edited snapshot is prose again, and prose is what this file exists to kill.

## Why this file exists (TDW_04 B0, F-04.22 / F-04.23)

The `engine` schema had **no documented DDL anywhere**. The migration ladder (`db/migrations/` 0001–0074) is **public-only** — it never creates an engine table. `docs/SCHEMA.md` documents the **public** schema; its `messages` table is `public.messages` (17 columns, the WhatsApp shape), **not** `engine.messages` (6 columns). So every engine-side query any session wrote was guesswork against prod.

It cost twice, in two different blocks, both times in SQL a founder had to run by hand:
- **A4:** `vendor_activity_log.detail` — truth: `summary`.
- **B0:** `agent_snapshot.rebuilt_at` — truth: **a key inside the `note` jsonb**, not a column. See `agent_snapshot` below: the default is `'{"items": [], "rebuilt_at": null}'::jsonb`. **The reference disproves the guess in its own second table.**

## The standing rule this enables (CE-ruled 2026-07-15 — binds every session, including the one that wrote it)

> **Founder-run SQL is written ONLY against witnessed column lists** — this file, `db/BASELINE.md`, `docs/SCHEMA.md` — **never against prose.**

## How to read it

- **Column counts reconcile with `db/BASELINE.md` for all 25 tables.** `usage` = **12**, confirming 02-HOTFIX F12's `cache_read_tokens`/`cache_write_tokens` DDL is applied in prod (BASELINE's count map predates it and says 10; its own note says 12).
- **Gaps in the numbering are NOT errors — they are dropped columns' fingerprints.** Five tables skip an ordinal: `documents`(9), `facts`(10), `leads`(11), `money_entries`(14), `open_loops`(12). Those are **exactly** the five tables migration **0074** dropped `scope_org_id` from. The ladder's applied history is legible in the schema itself.
- **Two-plane words live here — read the schema name, never the table name alone.** `engine.events` is an **agent audit trail**, NOT the calendar (`public.events` is the calendar). `engine.leads` is **stop-written and empty**; the live typed plane is `public.leads` (27 cols). `engine.messages` is the engine turn log; `public.messages` is WhatsApp. See `docs/SURFACE_TRUTH_AUDIT.md` §3.

---

## engine.agent_owner  ·  7 columns

```
1. agent_id uuid NOT NULL
2. owner_name text
3. owner_descriptor text
4. note text
5. consult_done boolean NOT NULL default false
6. created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL default now()
7. updated_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL default now()
```

## engine.agent_snapshot  ·  4 columns

```
1. agent_id uuid NOT NULL
2. note jsonb NOT NULL default '{"items": [], "rebuilt_at": null}'::jsonb
3. updated_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL default now()
4. created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL default now()
```

## engine.agents  ·  11 columns

```
1. id uuid NOT NULL default gen_random_uuid()
2. user_id uuid NOT NULL
3. display_name text
4. kind text NOT NULL default 'solo'::text
5. profession_preset text
6. tier text NOT NULL default 'entry'::text
7. timezone text NOT NULL default 'Asia/Kolkata'::text
8. created_at timestamp with time zone NOT NULL default now()
9. mode text NOT NULL default 'advisory'::text
10. cabinet_skin text
11. victor_mode text NOT NULL default 'business'::text
```
